name: Vegetable Fried Rice
steps: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wide pan over medium-high heat. Add diced tofu if using and cook for 4–5 minutes until lightly golden on the edges, then remove it to a plate.
Add the remaining 1 tbsp oil to the same pan. Add carrot and bell pepper and stir-fry for 3–4 minutes until the vegetables soften slightly but still look bright.
Add peas and mixed vegetables. Cook for 2–3 minutes, stirring often, until the vegetables are hot and any moisture has mostly evaporated.
Add the chilled cooked rice and break up clumps with the back of a spoon. Stir-fry for 3–4 minutes until the rice is hot and separate.
Pour soy sauce around the edges of the pan so it sizzles, then mix well. Add black pepper and a small pinch of salt.
Return the tofu to the pan if using. Toss everything for 1–2 minutes until evenly coated and lightly glossy.
Turn off the heat and fold in green onion. Sprinkle sesame seeds if available and serve hot.
cookTips: Use cold cooked rice because fresh rice can turn sticky.
Keep the heat medium-high so the rice fries instead of steaming.
Add soy sauce gradually because it already contains salt.

substitutions.note: Use extra salt lightly if soy sauce is unavailable, though the flavor will be simpler.
Use egg for a non-vegetarian version with extra protein.

servingSuggestions: Serve as a quick lunch bowl.
Serve with cucumber slices on the side.
Top with extra green onion for freshness.

name: Creamy Tomato Pasta
steps: Bring a large pot of water to a boil over high heat. Add salt and pasta, then cook for 8–11 minutes until tender but still slightly firm. Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water and drain.
While the pasta cooks, heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add onion and cook for 5–6 minutes until soft and translucent.
Add garlic and cook for 45–60 seconds, stirring constantly, until fragrant but not browned.
Add chopped tomatoes, oregano, chili flakes, black pepper, and a pinch of salt. Cook for 8–10 minutes until the tomatoes collapse and the sauce thickens.
Lower the heat. Stir in cream slowly and cook for 2–3 minutes until the sauce turns smooth and light orange.
Add cooked pasta and toss for 2 minutes, adding a splash of reserved pasta water if the sauce feels too thick.
Stir in parmesan. Add mozzarella if using and cook for 1 minute until it begins to melt.
Turn off the heat, tear basil over the top if available, and serve immediately.
cookTips: Reserve pasta water before draining because it helps loosen and bind the sauce.
Keep the heat low after adding cream to prevent splitting.
Cook the tomatoes until they look jammy for better flavor.

substitutions.note: Use milk with a little butter for a lighter sauce.
Use cheddar for stronger flavor or mozzarella for a milder, stretchy finish.

servingSuggestions: Serve with lettuce and cucumber salad.
Add extra black pepper before serving.

name: Chicken Rice Bowl
displayIngredients: 1 cup broccoli, cut into small florets
steps: Pat the chicken pieces dry and season them with salt, paprika, and black pepper.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wide pan over medium-high heat. Add the chicken in one layer and cook for 4–5 minutes without moving too much, until the underside browns.
Turn the chicken pieces and cook for another 4–5 minutes until cooked through and no longer pink in the center. Remove to a plate.
Add the remaining oil to the pan. Add garlic and ginger and cook over medium heat for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add carrot and broccoli with 2 tbsp water. Cover and cook for 4–5 minutes until the broccoli turns bright green and the carrot softens slightly.
Return the chicken to the pan. Add soy sauce and toss for 2 minutes until the chicken and vegetables are coated and glossy.
Spoon hot rice into bowls and top with the chicken and vegetables.
Finish with green onion and sesame seeds if available.
cookTips: Dry the chicken before cooking so it browns instead of steaming.
Cut broccoli small so it cooks quickly in the pan.
Do not overcook the chicken after returning it to the pan.

substitutions.note: Use snap peas or bell pepper for a quicker-cooking vegetable.
Use salt carefully and add a little extra water to prevent dryness.

servingSuggestions: Serve with cucumber slices for crunch.
Serve with extra rice for a larger meal.
Add hot sauce at the table if desired.
